plan.description: The project investigates the biological effects of dietary exposure to microplastics (MPs) and PFAS-adsorbed MPs in seabream, focusing on metabolism and reproductive health. It also explores the potential of dietary post-biotics as a mitigation strategy against the toxicity of these environmental contaminants. Experimental data will support early-warning systems and inform regulatory bodies and stakeholders to improve plastic and PFAS management, promoting sustainable and health-protective interventions.This DMP describes the management of biological, genomic and phenotypic data generated in the project. It will ensures compliance with FAIR principles, ethical standards, and AquaServ open data policies.
plan.title: PID 37349- Francesca Maradonna- PRISM-P

blueprint.label: AQUASERV DMP Blueprint v1

systemField.label: Title of Plan
systemField.description: Please provide a name for your DMP Plan

systemField.label: Description
systemField.description: Briefly describe the context and purpose of the DMP

systemField.label: Language
systemField.description: Select the language of your DMP

referenceField.label: Researchers
referenceField.description: Add here the names of people that have produced, processed, analysed the data described in the DMP.

referenceField.label: Organizations
referenceField.description: Add here the names of the organizations contributing to the creation and revision of the DMPs

extraField.label: Introduction
extraField.description: Ethical considerations in science become crucial whenever your research involves potential harm to living organisms, ecosystems, or communities. If your research fits into these criteria please answer following questions. As a default please fill the Horizon Europe - Ethics template, however other templates are available.

extraField.label: Introduction
extraField.description: Please select a template to describe your dataset that best fits your research topic. You can chose more than one template. All the templates prepared for the AQUASERV project are called "AQUASERV | category | datatype". If you can't find a template that fits your research, please contact aquaservhelpdesk@ualg.pt

blueprint.description: This is the main AQUASERV blueprint containing common questions about you and your project, as well as multiple "templates." The common questions need to be answered only once. Use one "template" for each dataset you are generating. For example, if you generate genomics data, use the genomics template (already available); if you generate LC-MS data, use the LC-MS template (coming soon). If you generate more than one type of dataset, you can combine multiple templates into one DMP "plan."

blueprintValue.value: Sample collection and processing complies with EU animal welfare regulations. Animal manipulation complies with the guidelines of the European Union Directive (2010/63/EU) and the Italian Legislative Decree 26 of March 4, 2014. The general objective of the project is to assess how the ingestion of MPs through the diet may affect the health status of gilthead seabream, a commercially important species of high economic value and among the most demanded, both wild-caught and farmed. The aim is therefore to analyze how MPs, once adsorbed with environmental pollutants, can interfere at both metabolic and reproductive levels and may also affect fillet quality, as well as how the co-administration of a postbiotic could mitigate their potential toxicity. Thus considery the complexity, the work couldn't be carried out without conducting experiments or other scientific procedures on living animals.
This data management plan will outline the results of study PID 37349 - PFAS, Microplastics, and Seabream Health: Assessing Risks and the Protective Role of Postbiotics

reference.label: Università Politecnica delle Marche, Dipartimento Scienze della Vita e dell'Ambiente
Institute of Marine Biology, Biotechnology & Aquaculture/Hellenic Centre for Marine Research

description.description:
This data management plan will outline the results of study PID 37349 - PFAS, Microplastics, and Seabream Health: Assessing Risks and the Protective Role of Postbiotics.
The datasets to be generated from the project are

- omic data  from gonads, liver, brain, muscle
- qPCR validation data
description.label: AQUASERV | Molecular Biology & Omics | sequencing

descriptionTemplate.label: AQUASERV | Molecular Biology & Omics | sequencing
descriptionTemplate.description: DMP template targeted for TA users generating genomics datasets

fieldSet.title: Sequencing chemistry / kits used
fieldSet.description: Which kits were used to generate your data? (e.g. Nanopore kit V14)

section.description: What is the type of the sequencing machine used to generate your data (e.g. Oxford Nanopore, Illumina Miseq)
section.title: Sequencing machine details

fieldSet.title: additional information about your dataset
fieldSet.description: please describe here your dataset if needed

section.description: What is the type of your data? (e.g. amplicon, WGS, transcriptomics)
section.title: General type of data

section.description:
What types of data will be created and/or collected, in terms of data
format and amount/volume of data?
section.title: Type of data (format)

fieldSet.title: How will the data be documented and described/analysed? Please specify which type of documentation record are you planning to use (e.g. paper or electronic labbbok, online resource) and which software are you planning to analyse your data with

section.description:
How will the data be documented and described/analyzed?
Please specify which type of documentation record you plan to use (e.g., paper or electronic lab notebook, online resource) and which software you plan to use to analyze your data.
section.title: Documentation and data quality

fieldSet.title: Do you use any intermediate storage devices? Please describe your backup plan.

section.description: Do you use any intermediate storage devices? Please describe your backup plan.
section.title: Storage and Backup

fieldSet.title: Long term data storage - database
fieldSet.description: which database will you use (e.g. genbank, ebi-ena)

fieldSet.title: Accession number
fieldSet.description: Please provide your accession number generated for your project

section.description: How, when, and where will research data or metadata be made accessible?
section.title: Accessibility and long-term storage

fieldSet.title: Metadata format
fieldSet.description: what is the format of your metadata (i.e. excel spreadsheet)

fieldSet.title: How is your metadata connected to the research data
fieldSet.description: Can you be certain that the samples data and metadata can be connected? Explain your strategy here

fieldSet.title: Metadata storage
fieldSet.description: Where do you store your metadata? For example, Zenodo allows for file submission—please provide the URL to your metadata.

section.description: Will you be producing and storing metadata?
section.title: Your metadata

fieldSet.title: Under what license will you publish your research data?
fieldSet.description:
Common licenses used in science include:

    Creative Commons Licenses:

        CC BY (Attribution): Requires users to give appropriate credit when reusing data or publications.

        CC0 (Public Domain Dedication): Allows data to be used without restrictions, effectively placing it in the public domain.

section.description: Under what license will you publish your research data?
section.title: Licence

section.description: If you submit your data to GenBank or EBI-ENA, will you choose to delay its publication? If so, what are your reasons for doing so? The expectation for the EU-funded project AQUASERV is that data will be publicly available as soon as it is generated.
section.title: Embargo on data release

field.textValue:
Yes, I can be certain that the sample data and metadata can be reliably connected. My strategy relies on using a unique, persistent identifier assigned to each sample at the moment of collection (e.g., a code or barcode). This identifier is recorded consistently across every step of the workflow, including sample labeling, data entry, and metadata acquisition.
To ensure traceability:
Each sample receives a unique ID that is never reused.
The same ID is used in all data files, metadata sheets, and analytical outputs.
Data entry is standardized through templates to minimize human error.
Periodic cross-checks are performed to confirm that the identifiers match across data and metadata sources.
This approach ensures a secure and unambiguous link between each physical sample and its associated data and metadata.
This will decided as soon as the data analysis will start (january-february 2026)
The data will be available and accessible at the time the scientific MS will be submitted, approximately mid 2026.
data will be publicly available as soon as they will be pubblished

1.taxonomic profiling of microbial communities from shotgun metagenomic short-read data 2. RNA-seq data to generate gene-level quantification, perform differential expression analysis (DEA), and provide functional interpretation.

Metagenome Sequencing: ○ Shotgun library preparation ○ Illumina NovaseqX, 2x150, 9Gbp output per samples
Eukaryotic mRNA sequencing ○ PolyA enrichment ○ Library prep ○ Sequencing: Illumina NovaseqX, 2x150, 12Gb output per sample

The project will generate both raw data and processed data, as well as metadata describing the samples and analytical procedures. The main types of data include:
Raw experimental data, (typically in xlsx, txt .png, FASTAQ, TIFF) produced directly by the instruments.
Processed and analyzed data, stored in .xlsx /.pdf formats after quality-control and computational analysis.
Metadata, including sample identifiers, collection information, and experimental conditions, stored in structured formats such as .xlsx, .csv, or standardized metadata templates.

Metagenomics data will be analysed -Alpha diversity will be analyzed using non parametric statistics. Beta diversity and dissimilarity heatmaps will be quantified using Bray-Curtisdissimilarity or other metrics. PCoAPCoA or NMDS is applied to visualize inter-sample variation. PERMANOVA tests are used to determine whether microbial communities differ significantly across metadata-defined groups. Abundance comparisons are conducted using count-based or compositional methods (e.g., DESeq2, ALDEx2).Gene-Level Quantification and Normalization: Reads are assigned to genomic features to generate a raw gene count matrix. Counts are further processed toobtain TPM, FPKM, and TMM-normalized matrices.Clustering and Exploratory Visualization. Sample relationships are explored using dimensionality reduction techniques such as Multidimensional Scaling (MDS), Principal Component Analysis (PCA), or Principal Coordinates Analysis (PCoA) to assess potential batch effects or outliers. Differential Expression Analysis. Differentially expressed genes (DEGs) are identified based on the experimental design. Results are provided in Excel tables, along with visualizations such as Volcano plots, MA plots, and Heatmaps. Gene Ontology Enrichment Analysis. DEGs are analyzed for enrichment of biological functions, focusing on Gene Ontology (GO) terms. Results are provided in Excel tables and summarized through dot plots, bar plots, treemaps, and enrichment maps.
Shotgun metagenomic, Taxonomic profiling, short read alignement-based Bulk RNAseq differential expression analysis (Genome-Based)

description.description: Sample collection and processing complies with EU animal welfare regulations. Animal manipulation complies with the guidelines of the European Union Directive (2010/63/EU) and the Italian Legislative Decree 26 of March 4, 2014. The general objective of the project is to assess how the ingestion of MPs through the diet may affect the health status of gilthead seabream, a commercially important species of high economic value and among the most demanded, both wild-caught and farmed. The aim is therefore to analyze how MPs, once adsorbed with environmental pollutants, can interfere at both metabolic and reproductive levels and may also affect fillet quality, as well as how the co-administration of a postbiotic could mitigate their potential toxicity. Thus considery the complexity, the work couldn't be carried out without conducting experiments or other scientific procedures on living animals.
description.label: Horizon Europe - Ethics

descriptionTemplate.label: Horizon Europe - Ethics
descriptionTemplate.description: A template of Horizon Europe about ethics.

fieldSet.title: Are there, or could there be, any ethics or legal issues that can have an impact on data sharing? These can also be discussed in the context of the ethics review. If relevant, include references to ethics deliverables and ethics chapter in the Description of the Action (DoA).
Will informed consent for data sharing and long term preservation be included in questionnaires dealing with personal data?
